Operational reliability: uptime not given per validator; we have current_index and partial false. Missed ledgers indicate downtime. For v001 etc missed small. v006 and v010 have zero agreement (offline). So they are down.

We need to assign scores 0-100.

Base scoring: high consensus performance is primary. So we can map agreement_30d_score to base.

Define thresholds: >0.998 => excellent (score 90+). 0.995-0.998 => good (80-89). 0.99-0.995 => moderate (70-79). 0.97-0.99 => lower (60-69). <0.97 => poor (below 60). Also consider 24h and 1h.

Also penalize those with 0 agreement (offline) heavily (<30). Also low 30d like 0.95-0.97 maybe 50-60.

Add bonuses for verified domain (+2-5). Add diversity: need to consider country distribution. We don't have per validator country; but many validators likely in US, Germany etc. Those with domains indicating maybe US? Hard. We'll ignore diversity for now, maybe small bonus for underrepresented domains (e.g., .xyz maybe unknown). But we can give small +1 for validators with verified domain.

Also fee votes all same (10) so neutral.

Now compute.
